entry.title: iniBuilds Updates A300-600R Airliner for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has recently issued an update to their A300-600R Premium Airliner for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, improving systems, performance, visuals, sounds, and overall usability. Update v1.0.7 delivers significant performance gains across a wide range of systems, from low-end to high-end. On their low-spec test system with a mobile RTX 4070, the passenger version went from 26.8 FPS to 46.8 FPS, and the freighter went from 30.7 FPS to 43.6 FPS, with stability improvements. On their high-end test system with a 9800X3D and an RTX 4090, the frames per second on the passenger version went from 64.7 FPS to 80.8 FPS, and on the freighter version from 75.7 FPS to 84.3 FPS. They have also implemented numerous autopilot and flight mode fixes, added pause at top of descent, time compression, CPDLC compatibility for BATC/SI, improved EFB startup options, and resolved a variety of art, LOD, and sound issues. The complete changelog follows below: ‍ The A300-600R Premium is available on their store for roughly $60, requiring at least 3.5 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Aerosoft Shares New A340-600 Screenshots for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Aerosoft has shared new screenshots of their A340-600 Pro for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020/2024, developed in conjunction with ToLiss, a renowned X-Plane add-on developer with a vast lineup of high-fidelity Airbus aircraft. Formerly the world’s longest commercial aircraft (up until the arrival of the Boeing 747-8), the A340-600 was designed to compete with early jumbo jets that were getting phased out at the time due to their age, offering the same four engines and similar passenger carrying capabilities, but boasting full fly-by-wire technology. While the range was nothing short of impressive, and the flying experience was smooth and comfortable, the timing was far from perfect, since the early 2000s also brought a beefy long-haul widebody with two engines, the 777-300ER, which was fairly cheaper to operate and ended up being the airplane of choice for many operators around the globe. Aerosoft says things are “moving quickly” with the project and that the upcoming week will be very exciting for everyone waiting for the product. The riddle they teased recently will be solved, and they can’t wait to share what they have been working on. Furthermore, they shared a fresh batch of screenshots that show a bit more of the A340-600's interior and exterior. There’s still no information about the pricing or the eventual release date, but Threshold will keep you updated! If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: 29Palms Releases Chania International Airport for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. 29Palms has recently released their rendition of Chania International Airport (LGSA) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, serving the city of Chania, Crete, with an average of 4 million passengers per year. The airport was originally a military base, and commercial flights were transferred from the previous airport (Maleme) due to insufficient capacity in the late 1950s, which eventually plagued the new airport as well, leading to the construction of a new terminal building in the 1990s. The new terminal was inaugurated in 1996, with a surface area of 14,650 square meters and six aircraft stands, designed to handle up to 1.35 million passengers per year. The airport was privatized in 2015 and is now operated by Fraport AG and the Copelouzos Group for the next 40 years, as of April 2017. In mid-2018, the airport saw its first post-privatization upgrades, including an expansion of the passenger safety area, ground layout changes, twice as many hand baggage scanners, new boarding gates, and more. It’s a focus city for Aegean Airlines and Ryanair. Air Serbia, Animawings, Austrian Airlines, British Airways, Brussels Airlines, Condor, Discover Airlines, easyJet, Edelweiss, Eurowings, Finnair, Jet2.com, Luxair, Neos, Norwegian Air Shuttle, Scandinavian Airlines, Sky Express, Smartwings, Transavia, TUI Airways, TUI fly Belgium, TUI fly Netherlands, and Wizz Air also serve it. The scenery features an accurate rendition of the airport, with custom ground textures, highly detailed 3d objects, terminal interiors, sloped runway profile, custom animated wingsocks, and much more. It’s available on Contrail for roughly $18.58, requiring at least 2.32 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Aerosoft/ToLiss A340-600 Pro Trailer Released
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Aerosoft has published the official trailer for the Airbus A340-600 Pro, its joint project with ToLiss for MSFS 2020 and MSFS 2024. The video spotlights the EFB, failures, a taxi camera view, and what is described as a custom flight and engine model. Aerosoft has not attached a release date to the trailer, so, for now, this is still a preview, not a launch announcement. Also part of today’s announcement is the embargo lift, as Aerosoft has cleared a first wave of creators to start streaming the A340-600 Pro from 1300Z on March 7. You can now see the aircraft in action on YouTube. We will, of course, follow the project closely, leading up to release. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: iniBuilds Update A340 To v1.0.10
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. IniBuilds have just launched their latest update for the A340 Airliner. This update focuses on various improvements which improve operational accuracy and refining several core aircraft systems. They have stated that multiple aircraft systems including flight guidance, FMGS behaviours, navigation handling and MCDU functionality have been fixed and improved. The ground handling integration for the large jet has also been improved. We also see the addition of a new noise cancelling option in the EFB along with fixed cockpit sounds and enhanced cabin environment sounds. For the full change log follow the link: https://forum.inibuilds.com/topic/35789-inibuilds-a340-airliner-for-microsoft-flight-simulator-2024-v1010-released/ ‍

entry.title: BlueBird Simulations Release Brief 757 200 Update
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Arguably the most anticipated airliner for MSFS, the BB 757, has been further teased in a brief update on the development groups discord channel. We are shown two lovely wing view screenshots along with a brief update on how the project is progressing. The developer informed us that the -200 variant is undergoing some finishing touches and additional details are being added which in turn hints that it could be getting close to release! The bluebird 757 is set to be one of the most in depth airliners to come to the platform in recent times and if you would like to see more about it check out our previous articles covering update videos from the development cycle. Threshold will keep you updated as the aircraft develops ‍

entry.title: Nattani RAF Valley Freeware Scenery Updated For MSFS2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. The Popular freeware rendition of EGOV has been taken over by developer Nattani and has been updated and enhanced for MSFS 2024 and is now available via Flightsim.to Well and truly a group project this msfs2020 scenery has been brought back to life by a group of dedicated devs including to mention a few: Deadjen and NickB with contributions from UK2000’s Gary. The new version of the scenery includes: ‍

entry.title: FlightFX Announce Piaggio Update With MSFS2024 Release Date and More
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. FlightFX today announced a major milestone for one of its most distinctive aircraft products, now officially titled the Piaggio P.180 Avanti II. Known for its unmistakable design and unique twin pusher-prop configuration, the aircraft stands out for blending speed, style, and advanced engineering in a way few aircraft do. FlightFX’s rendition for MSFS 2024 brings that character into Microsoft Flight Simulator with advanced flight and engine modeling, ProLine 21 avionics, VAMS 2.0 tablet integration, and highly detailed visual and audio work built from on-site reference material. FlightFX confirmed that the Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024 version of the Piaggio P.180 Avanti II has completed active development and testing, with a release date of March 10, 2026. In addition, some updates will also be released for the 2020 version as well. This release also marks the aircraft’s official designation as a licensed product. As part of this milestone, FlightFX will be updating product naming and branding across both of its Microsoft Flight Simulator releases. Users will see the Piaggio P.180 Avanti II name reflected in the Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 version as well, aligning the product with its official licensed designation. “Official licensing is an important step for this aircraft and for our community,” said Nick Sdoucos, CEO at FlightFX. “It reflects our commitment to representing the Piaggio P.180 Avanti II accurately, partnering properly, and continuing to refine the experience through meaningful updates over time.” The Piaggio P.180 Avanti II will be available on the in-game marketplace on MSFS 2020 and 2024. Owners of the current version in MSFS 2020 will be entitled to a free upgrade to the 2024 version. Please note that users are advised to purchase the aircraft in the sim they prefer to use first, as entitlements can sometimes take a brief period of time before syncing between the two platforms. Key highlights include: ● Now officially licensed as the Piaggio P.180 Avanti II ● Name and branding updates coming to the Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 version ● Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024 version releases March 10, 2026 ● Continued support and ongoing updates planned ‍

entry.title: Aerosoft Announces March 12 Release for A340-600 Pro
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Aerosoft has confirmed that the Airbus A340-600 Pro will be released on March 12, 2026, with the date revealed during a recent developer stream on Aerosoft’s Twitch channel, hosted by community manager Rafi. The A340-600 Pro is a joint effort between Aerosoft and ToLiss, with Aerosoft focusing on the aircraft’s modelling and presentation, and ToLiss handling the underlying systems. In the past week, Aerosoft has steadily ramped up the public-facing coverage, first with an official trailer, also lifting the preview embargo, giving content creators the green light to show the aircraft ahead of release. We'll of course keep you updated. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: FlightFactor Releases Interactive Procedures for the 777 in X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. X-Plane developer FlightFactor, has released another update for their 777 series in X-Plane 12. This time, the update is quite a major addition, with the inclusion of interactive procedures. The interactive procedures aim to bring further immersion and realism into the 777, bringing realistic SOP’s (standard operating procedures) to the simulator. In regards to how this works, the user will act as captain, and be able to activate various procedures for the FO to perform through the EFB. Furthermore, voice commands can be used, such as requesting “gear up,” where the FO will then comply. The feature list is immense, and there are various different parts to the interactive procedures, therefore, to view more information, head over to the forum post by FlightFactor where they talk about it in-depth. FlightFactor also included other features in this update, including various bugfixes, and other minor additions. The full feature list can be seen below To purchase the FlightFactor 777, head over to the x-plane.org store. The base package (777-200ER), can be purchased for $89.00, and various other expansions such as the extended engine package, the 777F, and a bundle can be purchased as well.

entry.title: Aerosoft/ToLiss Release Airbus A340-600 Pro for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Aerosoft and ToLiss have recently released their rendition of the Airbus A340-600 for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, bringing a faithful recreation of the world’s former longest commercial aircraft with a distinctive set of features. Formerly the world’s longest commercial aircraft (up until the arrival of the Boeing 747-8), the A340-600 was designed to compete with early jumbo jets that were getting phased out at the time due to their age, offering the same four engines and similar passenger carrying capabilities, but boasting full fly-by-wire technology. While the range was nothing short of impressive, and the flying experience was smooth and comfortable, the timing was far from perfect, since the early 2000s also brought a beefy long-haul widebody with two engines, the 777-300ER, which was fairly cheaper to operate and ended up being the airplane of choice for many operators around the globe. The joint effort between Aerosoft (3D model) and ToLiss (systems) promises one of the most authentic Airbus simulations on the market, with a custom flight model featuring proper aerodynamic effects of each control surface, accurate lift and drag modelling, effects of control surface failures on the dynamics and drag computations, custom coded flight control laws representing normal law, alternate one, alternate two, and direct law, with their respective differences in handling and available protections. It also features a detailed model of the hydro-mechanical and electrical systems, 303 different system failures that can be injected via a fault injection screen, or completely randomly at the user’s choice, functional reset switches, Hoppie CPDLC support, ACARS functionality with SimBrief flight plans uplinking directly into the FMGS, and take-off performance computations via uplink request, and a detailed custom display system with all ECAM screens, PFD, and MFD. Furthermore, it sports three functional - and independent - MCDUs, two completely independent autopilots, an advanced flight management system with support for ARINC 424 legs, Airbus-specific VNAV with step climbs, route offsets, pilot-defined holdings, computed holdings, and database holdings, custom waypoints, custom navaids, custom runways, and more. The ADIRU system is completely custom-coded and includes injection of measurement accuracy errors on pressure readings and attitude angles, simulating small differences between the pilot and copilot sides as per real life. On top of that, the engine model is completely custom, achieving high accuracy in simulated engine thrust and, consequently, its performance. The Electronic Flight Bag provides two sets of functions: the top row shows functions found in real life, and the bottom row is simulator-specific. There’s Navigraph integration for the map, navdata updates, and charts; an OFB display (SimBrief); a weight-and-balance screen; takeoff and landing performance calculations; and an interactive checklist. As for the bottom row, it has situation saving and reinstating, autosave, aircraft state change, quick engine starts, forcing alternate or direct law, choice between imperial or metric, etc. The complete feature list for the aircraft follows below: ‍A340-600Pro FeaturesAircraft handling Aircraft systems – general Aircraft systems – FMGS suite Aircraft systems – specific Useability Visuals Included situationsThese 10 situations allow you to explore the aircraft's various levels of simulation. You can experience everything from an 'easy' approach to complicated system failures, where you, as the pilot, have to handle the situation correctly. Let's challenge yourself – it's awesome and great fun to experience what real pilots are trained for! Included liveries It’s available on Aerosoft’s website for roughly $80.75, requiring at least 18 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Laminar Research Pushes X-Plane 12.4.1 Into Public Beta
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. Laminar Research has just released X-Plane 12.4.1 as a public beta, bringing even more changes across the sim. The headline aircraft work continues on the default Airbus A330-300, which now includes a fully simulated, visibly deploying Ram Air Turbine, plus TCAS refinements with new aural warnings, and a long list of smaller logic fixes, ranging from vertical speed behaviour, to cockpit time display. Avionics and weather also see targeted upgrades, with the X1000 gaining a weather radar overlay on the MFD map, and Laminar tweaking real-world weather interpretation to reduce abrupt shifts, and improve overall fidelity, including reported altimeter-related edge cases. On the scenery side, new library assets arrive in the form of European-style cab-over trucks, and refreshed gravel textures, while 310 updated Gateway airports are included in the build. Under the hood, Laminar says XP12 12.4.1 includes further CPU-side optimisation work, claiming a 15 to 20 percent uplift in typical scenarios, alongside general stability improvements. As this is a beta, Laminar is clear that it can be unstable, and points users to the full release notes for the complete change log, and important caveats. Read the full update log here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Orbx Releases Oslo Gardermoen for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Orbx has released a new rendition of Oslo Gardermoen (ENGM) for MSFS 2024, developed by Finn Hansen and Jakub Lukaszewski. Across the airport, users can expect interior terminal modelling, updated photoreal ground textures, and 4K PBR materials, alongside a suite of custom ground equipment. Orbx also includes animated service traffic, covering pushback, buses, and other airside vehicles, and notes support for VDGS at applicable stands, which requires the Nool plugin. Oslo Gardermoen (ENGM) for MSFS 2024 is now available, priced at $21.23. MSFS 2020 customers can upgrade for free via Orbx Central. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Just Flight Publishes New A300B4 Development Update
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Just Flight has recently published a new development update (the first so far in 2026) on their rendition of the Airbus A300B4, showcasing some of the progress they have made, especially in the art department. While this development update may have been the first of the year, Just Flight has mentioned that the frequency of development updates will significantly increase from now on, as they are rapidly approaching the aircraft's beta testing phase. They have been putting a lot of effort into getting the lighting right by using video reference (hard to find since it’s such an old airplane), photos, FCOM information, and research trips to visit the real thing. In addition, they also simulated its many quirks and complexities, and spent many hours ensuring the intensity, color, and angles were correct. Furthermore, the A300B4 has some quirks that are pretty much unique to it, such as dedicated towing/park exterior navigation lights to help the ground crew, forward- and rear-facing navigation and strobe lights, separate wing and engine lights, and more. There is no release date or pricing information yet, but Threshold will keep you informed! If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: iniBuilds Updates A350 Airliner for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has just released an update for their A350 Airliner for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024. This new update brings the aircraft to version 1.2.3. The primary focus of the update is on improving system stability, navigation logic, and overall usability. It also includes various other fixes, improvements, and changes. The update comes with a comprehensive changelog, which is detailed below: ‍ V1.2.3 SYSTEMS ⚙️ FIXED WASM crash after selecting STAR FIXED TCAS triggering from aircraft which are non factors FIXED Pitch jerkiness when passing through 10° with constant stick input FIXED Manually entered CRS shows in small font in the A350 FIXED TCAS returns all defined as "HEAVY" FIXED D-ATIS not timing out FIXED Inaccurate colours on SEC/PERF/APPR page FIXED OPT FL should be shown every 500ft FIXED Waypoint revision logic on Active, Temporary, and Wind pages FIXED Position/Report page formatting issues FIXED Flightplan STEP ALTs page logic/format FIXED Time marker should be in chronological order FIXED Font & sizing issues on PERF approach page FIXED Key input not masked anymore in simconnect FIXED Loadsheet formatting FIXED Flight Director sensitivity FIXED PDC response not being received from HOPPIE connections FIXED Dest ETA/ Wpt ETO not updating correctly (same time for multiple waypoints) FIXED Cost Index logic FIXED Letter "D" missing from ANF font FIXED HUD not displaying all the information all the time / Switch start state FIXED VD - missing magenta area FIXED Waypoint selection menu not showing for duplicates FIXED Inserting CMS by writing the waypoint name on ACTIVE/F-PLN/VERT REV/CMS page not working as expected. FIXED Moving thrust levers from CL to MCT detent & back to CL detent during CLB FMS now cancels derated thrust. FIXED Spoiler panel jitters rapidly while deployed in flight FIXED Misspelling waypoint in DATABASE WPTS page causes the page to go blank FIXED Constraints & altitudes below the TL be shown in feet instead of FLs FIXED ECAM AIR PACK 1(2) OFF fault error when performing packs off takeoff FIXED Inconsistencies in the display of planned speed on the CRZ/FPLN pages FIXED Trim not resetting correct direction after landing FIXED RA logic on touchdown FIXED PLACE DISTANCE WAYPOINT not working with negative distances FIXED "NONE" appearing in the middle of the procedure list instead of at the top FIXED SURV Controls - Switching the ADS-B RPTG should not switch the ADS-B TRAFFIC FIXED Inserting "1" as a CRZ FL on the ACTIVE/PERF/CRZ page should insert the current altitude as an CRZ altitude FIXED VD planned path incorrect given altitude restrictions. FIXED Sec airways inserting double airways when no intersection found FIXED RWY CONDITION / BRAKING not appearing on SD FIXED Step descent logic FIXED Return button behaviour on Step alt page FIXED ECAM COMPANY MSG memo for TELEX messages not clearing (internal only) FIXED Range Switching behaviour from ROSE/PLAN to ARC modes & vice versa FIXED Multiple amber arc indicating hottest wheel brake ‍ ART 🎨 FIXED Cabin windows flickering FIXED Cabin lighting turns off instead of dimming on "medium" & "dim" settings FIXED No difference between low & medium dim in cabin modes ‍ SOUND 🔊 FIXED RA callouts refined across all tracks/volume and attenuation balanced per community feedback FIXED Tie APU external sound to "external engines" volume slider FIXED Quiet exterior sounds in 2020 FIXED Refined gear extension sounds in cockpit and cabin when landing FIXED "Autobrake Off" callout not sounding after touchdown The latest update is now available to all current owners. If you do not own the aircraft, it can be purchased from the iniBuilds Store for approximately $80.37. ‍ For the latest flight simulation news, remember to stay tuned on Threshold! ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: CFSS Releases Boundary Bay Airport for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. CFSS has released a new rendition of Boundary Bay Airport for MSFS 2024, bringing one of Canada’s busiest general aviation airports and a major flight training hub into the sim. Located just south of Vancouver, CZBB is known more for business jets, turboprops, maintenance traffic, and training flights than airliner operations, and that focus is reflected in the scenery package. According to CFSS, the airport has been recreated using extensive research, with accurately modelled FBOs, hangars, terminal areas, aprons, and taxiways, all designed to reflect the real-world layout. The developer also says the scenery uses current charts for markings and lighting, which should make it particularly well-suited to regular GA flying.‍ The scenery requires MSFS 2024, along with CFSS Library V1.0.0 or later. You can purchase the scenery for USD $14.55 here. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!
